name: Three Sisters
description: Triple linked SVF filter. Three tuned peaks (LOW, CENTRE, HIGH) share a master cutoff and spread. Switches between 24dB crossover and three-bandpass formant responses; QUALITY sweeps through notch to self-oscillation.

controls.name: FREQ
controls.description: Master cutoff. Shifts all three filter peaks in parallel across ~5 octaves.
controls.detail: v/8 tracking · adds to FREQ CV jack

controls.name: SPAN
controls.description: Pushes LOW down and HIGH up around FREQ center. In crossover mode widens/narrows CENTRE passband; in formant mode CENTRE is unaffected.
controls.detail: Narrow: peaks converge · Wide: peaks separate across spectrum

controls.name: QUALITY
controls.description: Bipolar resonance. CW increases Q to self-oscillation; CCW mixes inverted complementary filter outputs producing notch / anti-resonance.
controls.detail: CCW: notch · noon: dry · CW: resonate · ~3 o'clock: self-oscillates

controls.name: FM attenuverter
controls.description: Grey knob. Scales and inverts FM jack before summing to FREQ. Accepts audio-rate modulation.
controls.detail: CCW: invert · noon: zero · CW: unity

controls.name: Response switch
controls.description: Selects filter topology. CROSSOVER splits band into 24dB LP / BP / HP; FORMANT turns all three paths into 12dB bandpass for vowel-like spectra.

inputs.name: LOW (IN)
inputs.description: Signal into the LOW filter path only

inputs.name: CENTRE (IN)
inputs.description: Signal into the CENTRE filter path only

inputs.name: HIGH (IN)
inputs.description: Signal into the HIGH filter path only

inputs.name: ALL (IN)
inputs.description: Signal routed to all three filter paths in parallel

inputs.name: FM
inputs.description: Attenuverted modulation summed to FREQ. AC/DC coupled, audio-rate capable.

inputs.name: QUALITY
inputs.description: CV sums with QUALITY knob. ±5V at noon ≈ full sweep.

outputs.name: LOW (OUT)
outputs.description: LOW path. Crossover: 24dB lowpass at FREQ−SPAN. Formant: 12dB bandpass.

outputs.name: CENTRE (OUT)
outputs.description: CENTRE path. Crossover: bandpass bounded by LOW/HIGH cutoffs (VCA-like gate). Formant: 12dB bandpass at FREQ.

outputs.name: HIGH (OUT)
outputs.description: HIGH path. Crossover: 24dB highpass at FREQ+SPAN. Formant: 12dB bandpass.

outputs.name: ALL (OUT)
outputs.description: Equal-level passive mix of LOW/CENTRE/HIGH. Can clip with loud inputs or self-oscillating peaks.

behaviors.name: Crossover mode
behaviors.description: LOW becomes 4-pole (24dB) lowpass, HIGH becomes 4-pole highpass, CENTRE becomes a bandpass whose edges are LOW and HIGH cutoffs. SPAN controls the CENTRE passband width — at zero SPAN CENTRE closes entirely, acting as a SPAN-controlled VCA.

behaviors.name: Formant mode
behaviors.description: All three paths become 12dB/oct bandpass peaks. LOW and HIGH track FREQ ± SPAN; CENTRE tracks FREQ alone and is immune to SPAN. Sum via ALL(OUT) builds vowel-like resonant stacks; ideal for pinging with triggers.

behaviors.name: Anti-resonance / notch
behaviors.description: CCW half mixes each filter's complementary (inverted, 180° out of phase) output back into the signal. Full CCW produces deep notches at the peak frequencies — the inverse of the CW resonant response. Useful as a spectral duck or for subtractive timbre sculpting.

behaviors.name: Self-oscillation
behaviors.description: Each of the three peaks sings its own pitch. With nothing patched, tapping LOW/CENTRE/HIGH yields three sine voices tracked by FREQ v/8. In formant mode the three peaks can be pinged by triggers at ALL(IN) for additive percussive voices.

behaviors.name: v/8 pitch tracking
behaviors.description: FREQ jack is calibrated to 1V/octave over roughly five octaves. With QUALITY high enough to self-oscillate, Three Sisters becomes a three-voice sine oscillator. Intervals between the three peaks follow SPAN, making chord-like voicings from a single v/oct.

behaviors.name: ALL(IN) / ALL(OUT) parallel processing
behaviors.description: ALL(IN) fans the same signal into all three filters; ALL(OUT) is a passive equal mix of the three outputs. Patching ALL(IN) and taking individual outs splits one signal into three spectral bands; patching individual INs and taking ALL(OUT) is a three-channel spectral mixer.

behaviors.name: Audio-rate FM
behaviors.trigger: Audio source into FM with attenuverter open
behaviors.description: The FM jack is capable of audio-rate modulation, not just slow sweeps. Self-oscillating Three Sisters plus an FM carrier creates classic FM timbres. The FM attenuverter at full CCW produces symmetric negative-going modulation for through-zero-style character.

behaviors.name: Stackable ALL mix
behaviors.description: Officially supported passive mixing: stackable ALL(OUT) with other Three Sisters outputs or other modules on the same bus. No active mixer required; voltage summing occurs on the cable.

patchIdeas.name: Three-band parallel processor
patchIdeas.patch: Audio → ALL(IN) · Response: crossover · FREQ noon, SPAN wide · take LOW(OUT) to compressor, HIGH(OUT) to reverb, CENTRE(OUT) to dry mixer · recombine externally for multiband shaping without phase cancellation
patchIdeas.diagram:
Three Sisters:
* Response: crossover
* FREQ: noon · SPAN: wide

Mixer:

- Audio (Out) -> Three Sisters (ALL IN)
- Three Sisters (LOW OUT) -> Compressor (In)
- Three Sisters (CENTRE OUT) -> Mixer (In)
- Three Sisters (HIGH OUT) -> Reverb (In)
- Compressor (Out) -> Mixer (In)
- Reverb (Out) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Pinged formant chord
patchIdeas.patch: Response: formant · QUALITY ~2 o'clock · trigger burst → ALL(IN) · LOW/CENTRE/HIGH peaks ring as three-note chord · SPAN sets chord interval · FREQ plays the chord via v/8
patchIdeas.diagram:
Three Sisters:
* Response: formant
* QUALITY: ~2 o'clock (high Q)

- Trigger (Out) -> Three Sisters (ALL IN) // pings the peaks
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Three Sisters (FREQ)
- Three Sisters (ALL OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Three-voice sine oscillator
patchIdeas.patch: Nothing patched to inputs · QUALITY full CW (all three self-oscillate) · FREQ CV = v/oct from sequencer · SPAN sets interval between voices · LOW/CENTRE/HIGH = three sine voices · stack or mix for instant sine chord
patchIdeas.diagram:
Three Sisters:
* QUALITY: full CW (self-oscillating)
* SPAN: sets chord interval

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Three Sisters (FREQ)
- Three Sisters (LOW OUT) -> VCA 1 (In)
- Three Sisters (CENTRE OUT) -> VCA 2 (In)
- Three Sisters (HIGH OUT) -> VCA 3 (In)

patchIdeas.name: Dynamic spectral ducker
patchIdeas.patch: Audio → ALL(IN) · Response: crossover · SPAN at noon · QUALITY CCW for notch · LFO → QUALITY CV sweeps between notch and dry · creates a pulsing spectral hole that ducks the resonant frequency region on the beat
patchIdeas.diagram:
Three Sisters:
* Response: crossover
* QUALITY: CCW (notch)

LFO:

- Audio (Out) -> Three Sisters (ALL IN)
- LFO (Out) >> Three Sisters (QUALITY) // pulses notch depth
- Three Sisters (ALL OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: SPAN VCA gate
patchIdeas.patch: Audio → CENTRE(IN) · Response: crossover · SPAN at zero = CENTRE passband closes · envelope → SPAN (via attenuverter externally) opens/closes CENTRE as a rough VCA · QUALITY high shapes the gate's tone · percussive plucks with built-in resonance
patchIdeas.diagram:
Three Sisters:
* Response: crossover
* SPAN: starts at 0 (closed)

Envelope:

- Audio (Out) -> Three Sisters (CENTRE IN)
- Envelope (Out) >> Three Sisters (FREQ) // open gate via FREQ since SPAN has no CV
- Three Sisters (CENTRE OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Vowel morphing
patchIdeas.patch: Noise or sawtooth → ALL(IN) · Response: formant · QUALITY high · slow LFO → FREQ CV sweeps all three formants in parallel · SPAN set for vowel-like /a/–/i/ spacing · ALL(OUT) = morphing formant voice
patchIdeas.diagram:
Three Sisters:
* Response: formant
* QUALITY: high

LFO:
VCO:

- VCO (Saw) -> Three Sisters (ALL IN)
- LFO (Out) >> Three Sisters (FREQ)
- Three Sisters (ALL OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Audio-rate FM sines
patchIdeas.patch: QUALITY full CW (self-oscillating) · VCO saw → FM · FM attenuverter open ~2 o'clock · FREQ sets carrier pitch · three FM sines with slightly different peaks produce metallic cross-modulated tones · SPAN detunes voices for beating
patchIdeas.diagram:
Three Sisters:
* QUALITY: full CW
* FM attenuverter: ~2 o'clock

VCO:

- VCO (Out) -> Three Sisters (FM)
- Three Sisters (ALL OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Resonant drum body
patchIdeas.patch: Response: formant · QUALITY just below self-oscillation · short trigger → ALL(IN) · FREQ sets pitch · SPAN narrow for tom-like unified ping, wide for metallic bell · AD envelope → QUALITY CV for damping
patchIdeas.diagram:
Three Sisters:
* Response: formant
* QUALITY: ~2-3 o'clock
* SPAN: narrow for tom, wide for bell

Envelope:

- Trigger (Out) t> Three Sisters (ALL IN)
- Envelope (Out) >> Three Sisters (QUALITY) // damping
- Three Sisters (ALL OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Mid/side-style crossover split
patchIdeas.patch: Stereo sum → ALL(IN) · crossover mode · SPAN wide · LOW(OUT) + HIGH(OUT) → external sum as 'sides' · CENTRE(OUT) → 'mid' · independent processing of spectral extremes vs midrange
patchIdeas.diagram:
Three Sisters:
* Response: crossover
* SPAN: wide

Mixer:

- Audio (Out) -> Three Sisters (ALL IN)
- Three Sisters (LOW OUT) -> Mixer (Sides A)
- Three Sisters (HIGH OUT) -> Mixer (Sides B)
- Three Sisters (CENTRE OUT) -> Mixer (Mid)

patchIdeas.name: Notch-to-peak morph
patchIdeas.patch: Audio → ALL(IN) · QUALITY knob around noon · bipolar LFO → QUALITY CV · signal morphs between deep spectral notches (CCW excursion) and resonant peaks (CW excursion) at the same filter frequencies · vowel-like wah with negative resonance
patchIdeas.diagram:
Three Sisters:
* QUALITY: noon

LFO:

- Audio (Out) -> Three Sisters (ALL IN)
- LFO (Out) >> Three Sisters (QUALITY) // notch ↔ peak morph
- Three Sisters (ALL OUT) -> Output (In)
